sutta: once when the exalted 1 dwelt near banares in the deer palm at isi a number of elders who had returned who returned from arms gathering and eaten sat together in the round hall and this talk by chance arose this series was set by the exalted 1 in the way to the beyond in the questions of methiah who knows both ends and does not cling to the middle him i call a great man he here has passed beyond the seamstress and they said and what prey is the first end what's the second what's in the middle and who's the same stress i'll just stop here to comment this passage that they quoted the buddha is saying who knows both ends and does not cling to the middle him i call a great man he here has passed beyond the seamstress this was cedna in the in a suta in the sutani pata where many brahmins came to see the buddha and they asked questions so many questions and this was 1 of the things that the buddha mentioned and so these elder monks and they asked this question they wanted somebody to explain what is the first end what is the second and what's in the middle and who's the seamstress now after this had been said 1 of the monks answered the elders and said contact sirs is the first end its arising is the second its seizing is in the middle and craving is the seamstress for craving souls 1 just to this ever becoming birth indeed sirs to this extent among knows the noble comprehends the comprehensible and knowing the noble and the knowable comprehending the comprehensible he makes an end of ill or dukkha here and now and when he had does spoken another monk said the past is the first end the future is the second and present is in the middle and craving is the seamstress etc and he makes an end of ill and another said pleasure is the first end pain is the second indifference is in the middle and craving is the seamstress etc as before and another said name nama is the first end form rupa is the second consciousness is in the middle and craving is in is the seamstress and another said 1 sixth sense organs are the first end the 6 outer objects are the second consciousness is in the middle and craving is the seamstress and another said life's bundle is the first end it's arising is the second it's seizing is in the middle and craving is the seamstress for craving source 1 just to this ever becoming birth indeed sirs to this extent among knows the noble comprehends the comprehensible and knowing the knowable comprehending the comprehensible he makes an end of dukkha here and now and when he had finished speaking another monk addressed the elders and said we have all references replied as the matter appeared to each 1 of us let us go and visit the exalted 1 and tell him as the exalted 1 declares so will we bear in mind very well we joined the elders and they approached the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side and so seated they told him all the words and talk that had passed between them adding lord who spoke best each 1 of you monks in his own way spoke well but as to what i spoke of in the way to the beyond in mathias questions who knows both ends and does not cling to the middle he might call a great man he here has passed beyond the seamstress listen pay good heed i will speak yes lord they replied and the exalted 1 said verily contact monks is the first end its arising is the second its seizing is in the middle and craving is the seamstress or craving source a man just to this ever becoming birth early monks it is to this extent that the monk knows the noble comprehends the comprehensible and knowing the noble comprehending the comprehensible he makes an end of dukkha here and now that's the end of the suta
commentary:
[Music] this buddha said now that all the answers given by the monks were very good now but even though all of them were very good now yet his meaning was just as the first monk had spoken and this is 1 of those who does when you read this or you listen to it the first time you find it very difficult to understand it's 1 of those 2 tasks where you have to read it and again and think about it think many times about it and read and think about it and slowly and you'll find that the answers are very interesting another suta and i can finish once while the exalted 1 walked a tour among the kosalis with the great company of the sanghas monks he came to a kosalan town called danda kapaka and the exalted 1 stepped down from the road and sat on a seed made ready at the foot of a tree but the monks entered kapaka to seek a lodging now the verbal ananda with a number of monks went to the river achiravati to bathe his limbs and after he had bathed and had come out he stood in 1 garment drying his limbs then the monk approached the verbal ananda and said ananda raven sir was it after concentrating his whole mind that devadatta was declared by the exalted 1 gone we went hell bound for a kappa or an aeon or a wall cycle and panel unpardonable is devadatta or was it from some devastating i'll just stop here for a moment this manga had heard that the buddha had said now that the devadatta 1 of his disciples who had tried to harm the buddha had gone to hell for a while cycle and he was doubtful so he asked was it the the buddha knew it himself from from his mind reading the mind of devadatta or did he learn it from some deva this devadatta had cause kism in the sangha split the sangha and tried to kill the buddha and then later he died and so the buddha said now that he had gone to hell and then available ananda said it was even as the exalted 1 has declared now the now the member ananda approached the exalted 1 saluted and sat down at 1 side and so seated he told the exalted 1 all that had occurred then the exhorted 1 said either ananda that monk must be new not long gone forth or if an elder a witness 1 how when i have definitely declared it can there be an alternative i know not another person of whom this was declared by me after full mental concentration safe of ananda and so long an ananda as i saw a bright spot in devadata even the big end of a horse hair in size i declared not devadatta is wavered gone hell bound for a kappa unpardonable but it was when i saw none that i declared thus imagine ananda a cesspool of a man's death brim full of dung and a man had fallen in head and all and then a man appeared ready to help to do the friendly to set him in safety to lift him out yet were he to go all around the chess pool he would not see even the prick end of the horse hair of that man unsmeared by dung by which to grasp and lift him out and it is even so with devadatta ananda when i saw not a bright spot in him not even the big end of a horse hair in size that i declared gone wayward hellbound for a kalpa unpardonable is devadatta would you hear ananda the tattagata analyzing the feelings and thoughts of man this is the time of blessed 1 this is the time of well gone 1 for the exalted 1 to analyze men's feelings and thoughts the monks have been heard well buried in mine well listen ananda pay good heed i will speak yes lord we joined the verb ananda and the available and the exalted 1 said suppose ananda by mind encompassing mind i know this of some person there is both good and evil in him then presently by the same means i know the good has disappeared the evil is uppermost but the root of goodness is not cut off and from that good will proceed thus in future he is bound not to fall if ananda see neither split rotten nor spoiled by wind and heat by vital well seasoned be thrown on well till ground in a good field can you say for certain it will yield its growth increase and abundance yes surely lord even so ananda by mind encompassing mind i know of some person there is good and evil in him and then the good has disappeared the evil is uppermost but the root of goodness is not cut off and from that good will proceed thus he is bound not to fall in the future thoroughly ananda thus by mind encompassing mind the person of man is known to the tatar gaata thus by mind encompassing mind the feelings and thoughts of men are known to the tata gata thus by mine encompassing mind the future arising of things is known to the tattagata or supposed by mind encompassing mind i know the converse of some person in future he is bound to fall if seed neither split rotten and so forth be thrown on stony ground can you say for certain it will not yield its growth increase or abundance yes surely lord even so ananda i know of some person he is bound to fall thoroughly ananda thus by mine encompassing mind the person of man his thoughts and feelings and the future arising of things are known to the tatar gather then suppose i know of some person there's not a bright spot the size of a hairspray end in him and being utterly blank in his evil he will on the breaking up of the body after a death rise in the wayward way the ill way the abyss hell ananda if speed if sea split rotten spoiled by wind and heat be thrown on well till ground in a good field can you say for certain it will not yield growth increase or abundance yes surely lord even so ananda i know of some persons he will rise in hell thus my mind encompassing mind the person is known to the tata gata and so the i don't have time to finish all the types of persons that the buddha enumerated in this utah but they are not so important the thing about it is that this is 1 of those which shows that the buddha did predict that devadatta will go to hell that he is an evil person or he was an evil person and because of that he will suffer for a world cycle in hell but later books i try to say and that devadatta was a bodhisattva and and and he's not really suffering even though he has gone to hell but this contradicts the earliest uttasa as shown here so i'll stop here and if you have any questions or comments and we can discuss
